contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert bar to PSI?
contentBlocks.html:
Bar is a metric pressure unit equal to 100,000 pascals (100 kPa). PSI (pounds per square inch) is the standard pressure unit in the imperial system, used primarily in the US, UK, and other countries that use imperial measurements.

The conversion factor is: 1 bar = 14.5038 PSI. This is essential for tire pressure (European cars specify bar, US cars specify PSI), HVAC systems, industrial equipment, and scuba diving (tank pressure).

Tire pressure example: 2.2 bar = 31.9 PSI, 2.5 bar = 36.3 PSI, 3.0 bar = 43.5 PSI. Most passenger cars recommend 2.0–2.5 bar (29–36 PSI).

This converter calculates with full precision. All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

contentBlocks.title: How does bar to PSI conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
The conversion is based on the definition: 1 bar = 100,000 Pa, and 1 PSI = 6,894.757 Pa. Therefore, 1 bar = 100,000 / 6,894.757 ≈ 14.5038 PSI.

To convert bar to PSI, multiply by 14.5038. To convert PSI to bar, divide by 14.5038 (or multiply by 0.0689476).

The converter displays results with full precision. The bar-to-PSI conversion factor is constant and exact.

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• Standard atmospheric pressure: 1.01325 bar = 14.696 PSI.
  • Car tire pressure typically ranges from 2.0–2.5 bar (29–36 PSI).
  • Bicycle tires: road bikes 6–8 bar (87–116 PSI), mountain bikes 2–3 bar (29–44 PSI).
  • Scuba tanks: 200–300 bar (2,900–4,350 PSI) when full.

items.question: How many PSI is 1 bar?
items.answer: Exactly 14.5038 PSI. This is derived from the definition of bar (100,000 Pa) divided by PSI (6,894.757 Pa).

items.question: How many bar is 30 PSI?
items.answer: 30 PSI = 2.068 bar. This is a common tire pressure for passenger cars.

items.question: What is normal tire pressure in bar and PSI?
items.answer: Most passenger cars recommend 2.0–2.5 bar (29–36 PSI). Check the sticker on the driver's door jamb for your specific vehicle.

items.question: Is 1 bar the same as 1 atmosphere?
items.answer: Close but not exactly. 1 atmosphere = 1.01325 bar = 14.696 PSI. 1 bar is about 98.7% of 1 atmosphere.

items.question: What pressure do bicycle tires need?
items.answer: Road bikes: 6–8 bar (87–116 PSI). Mountain bikes: 2–3 bar (29–44 PSI). Hybrid/city bikes: 3–5 bar (44–73 PSI).

items.question: Are the calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
